findings.description: Exceptional CI/CD infrastructure with 18 GitHub Actions workflows covering comprehensive automation scenarios including tests, linters, coverage, security scanning, benchmarks, and PR quality checks.
findings.evidence: Workflow files detected in .github/workflows/: tests.yml, coverage_tests.yml, coverage_comment.yml, linters.yml, check_pr_quality.yml, check_commit_messages.yml, selenium.yml, postgis.yml, schedule_tests.yml, docs.yml, benchmark.yml, new_contributor_pr.yml, python_matrix.yml, screenshots.yml, labels.yml, check-migrations.yml, schedules.yml, and check_pr_quality.yml. tests.yml shows multi-platform testing (Windows, Ubuntu) with Python 3.14, JavaScript tests via npm, and script testing via unittest discovery.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining the comprehensive CI/CD pipeline. Consider periodic reviews to ensure workflows remain optimized and relevant.

findings.description: Repository shows very active and recent development with commits from April 2026, demonstrating ongoing maintenance and rapid issue resolution.
findings.evidence: Recent commits: commit 61a62be (April 22, 2026) 'Fixed #37057 -- Adjusted UniqueConstraint handling of UNKNOWN condition', commit 63c56cd (April 22, 2026) 'Fixed #35870 -- Made blank choice label in forms more accessible', commit dc467fd (April 22, 2026) 'Fixed #36991 -- Raised BadRequest for invalid encodings', commit 5cf9c7f (April 19, 2026) 'Refs #28586 -- Added DEFAULT_FETCH_MODE module constant'. All commits show proper attribution with multiple contributors (charettes, annalauraw, DineshThumma9, jacobtylerwalls).
findings.recommendation: Maintain current development velocity and continue the pattern of clear, well-documented commits with ticket references.

findings.description: Repository demonstrates exceptional code quality discipline with comprehensive linting tools and zero technical debt markers detected in Python codebase.
findings.evidence: Code quality configuration files present: .flake8 (297 bytes), .pre-commit-config.yaml (797 bytes), .editorconfig (697 bytes), biome.json (1699 bytes for JavaScript), tox.ini (2195 bytes). Search query 'repo:django/django TODO FIXME language:python' returned 0 results, indicating no TODO/FIXME markers in Python code.
findings.recommendation: Continue enforcing pre-commit hooks and automated linting. The zero TODO/FIXME result indicates excellent issue tracking discipline through Trac.

findings.description: Repository has accumulated several very old open pull requests (5+ years old), though this appears intentional for complex feature proposals requiring extended discussion and design work.
findings.evidence: Open PRs sampled: PR #11347 (opened May 9, 2019 - 5+ years old), PR #12438 (opened Feb 9, 2020 - 4+ years old, complex RegexpReplace implementation), PR #12712 (opened Apr 13, 2020 - 4+ years old), PR #14932 (opened Oct 3, 2021), PR #15592 (opened Apr 13, 2022 - FileField memory optimization with breaking changes discussion). PR quality workflow exists: check_pr_quality.yml (1686 bytes).
findings.recommendation: Consider adding PR age labels or documentation explaining the policy for long-running feature PRs. Establish clear communication about which PRs are awaiting design decisions vs. which need contributor action.

findings.description: Exemplary project organization with clear separation of source code, comprehensive test suites, documentation, and supporting infrastructure. Follows modern Python packaging standards.
findings.evidence: Root directory contains: django/ (source code tree), tests/ (test suite tree), js_tests/ (JavaScript tests tree), docs/ (documentation tree), scripts/ (utilities tree), extras/ (additional tools tree), .github/ (CI/CD workflows tree), pyproject.toml (2167 bytes - modern packaging), tox.ini (2195 bytes), package.json (367 bytes for Node.js), AUTHORS (45163 bytes), CONTRIBUTING.rst (1115 bytes), README.rst (2017 bytes), LICENSE (1552 bytes).
findings.recommendation: Maintain current structure. The clear separation and comprehensive documentation serve as an excellent model for other projects.

findings.description: Modern dependency management using PEP 517/518 standards with minimal core dependencies and clear separation of required vs optional dependencies. Uses pyproject.toml for declarative configuration.
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml specifies: build-system requires=['setuptools>=77.0.3'], requires-python='>= 3.12', core dependencies=['asgiref>=3.9.1', 'sqlparse>=0.5.0', 'tzdata; sys_platform==win32'], optional-dependencies with argon2=['argon2-cffi>=23.1.0'] and bcrypt=['bcrypt>=4.1.1']. Also has package.json (367 bytes) for JavaScript dependencies.
findings.recommendation: Continue using modern packaging standards. The minimal dependency footprint (only 2-3 core deps) reduces security surface and maintenance burden.

findings.description: Comprehensive multi-language testing infrastructure with dedicated test suites for Python, JavaScript, Selenium browser tests, and database-specific tests (PostGIS).
findings.evidence: tests.yml workflow shows: Windows testing with SQLite and Python 3.14, JavaScript tests via 'npm test', Python script tests via 'python -m unittest discover -v -s scripts/'. Additional workflows: selenium.yml (2714 bytes), postgis.yml (2271 bytes), coverage_tests.yml (3075 bytes). Separate test directories: tests/, js_tests/. Test requirements at tests/requirements/py3.txt.
findings.recommendation: Maintain comprehensive test coverage across all supported platforms and database backends. The current multi-faceted approach ensures high quality across the entire stack.

findings.description: Automated code coverage tracking with dedicated workflows for generating and commenting coverage reports on pull requests.
findings.evidence: coverage_tests.yml (3075 bytes) and coverage_comment.yml (2897 bytes) workflows present in .github/workflows/, indicating automated coverage reporting on pull requests.
findings.recommendation: Continue automated coverage reporting. Consider setting and documenting minimum coverage thresholds if not already established.

findings.description: Repository includes security scanning configuration and workflow security best practices including permission restrictions.
findings.evidence: zizmor.yml (383 bytes) present in root directory for security scanning. tests.yml workflow includes 'permissions: contents: read' and 'persist-credentials: false' in checkout steps, following GitHub Actions security best practices.
findings.recommendation: Continue security-first approach in CI/CD workflows. Regularly update security scanning tools and review findings.

findings.description: Strong community engagement evidenced by extensive contributor documentation, large contributor base, and clear contribution guidelines.
findings.evidence: AUTHORS file (45163 bytes - extensive contributor list), CONTRIBUTING.rst (1115 bytes), README.rst (2017 bytes), new_contributor_pr.yml workflow (1364 bytes) for welcoming new contributors, .readthedocs.yml (410 bytes) for documentation hosting.
findings.recommendation: Continue fostering community contributions. The automated new contributor workflow and comprehensive documentation lower the barrier to entry.

findings.description: Repository has a documented security policy that references the official Django Security Policy at djangoproject.com/security/
findings.evidence:
File .github/SECURITY.md exists (SHA: 58d191de54b8b93de9c67ebbc1fa0b5d183b2921) with content: '# Django Security Policies

Please see https://www.djangoproject.com/security/.'
findings.recommendation: Current implementation is appropriate. The delegation to the official Django security documentation is a best practice for mature projects with established security teams.

findings.description: No CodeQL or security-focused SAST tools detected in CI workflows. Repository uses code quality linters (flake8, black, isort) but lacks dedicated vulnerability scanners like CodeQL, Bandit, or Semgrep.
findings.evidence: Analyzed .github/workflows/linters.yml (SHA: ccee1adde022eccc84a9b95bce82294161d4212c) - contains flake8, black, isort, zizmor, and biome. Search query 'repo:django/django path:.github/workflows codeql' returned 0 results. No Bandit or Semgrep configurations found in 18 workflow files examined.
findings.recommendation: Enable GitHub CodeQL for Python and JavaScript to detect security vulnerabilities (SQL injection, XSS, code injection). Alternatively, add Bandit as a dedicated Python security linter in the linters.yml workflow.

findings.description: No automated dependency vulnerability scanning tool (Dependabot, Renovate) is configured for the repository.
findings.evidence: File check for .github/dependabot.yml returned error: 'does not exist in the repository'. Repository tree scan of .github directory (SHA: 8a6f92a4463d007caa298ed4c7eb1de5f5efad61) shows files: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, FUNDING.yml, SECURITY.md, copilot-instructions.md, pull_request_template.md, workflows/ - no dependabot.yml or renovate.json present.
findings.recommendation: Enable Dependabot for Python (tests/requirements/py3.txt and other requirements files) and JavaScript (package.json) dependencies. Configure security updates to run weekly and create automated PRs for vulnerable dependencies.

findings.description: Limited use of shell=True detected in utility scripts. Two occurrences found in controlled contexts (version management and translation scripts).
findings.evidence: Search query 'repo:django/django shell=True language:python' returned 2 results: django/utils/version.py (SHA: be727df7b6e37001c8074ad07b931e6634028f35) and scripts/manage_translations.py (SHA: ce3093ca4a51b327977c192634e5f26b40bd1148). Both are internal utility files, not user-facing code.
findings.recommendation: Review both files to ensure shell=True usage is necessary and that input sanitization is properly implemented. Consider refactoring to avoid shell=True where possible to reduce command injection attack surface.

findings.description: Excellent supply chain hygiene with SHA-pinned third-party GitHub Actions and security best practices in workflows.
findings.evidence: Workflow .github/workflows/linters.yml shows: zizmorcore/zizmor-action@e639db99335bc9038abc0e066dfcd72e23d26fb4 (SHA-pinned with comment v0.3.0), liskin/gh-problem-matcher-wrap@e7b7beaaafa52524748b31a381160759d68d61fb (SHA-pinned with comment v3.0.0). All workflows use 'persist-credentials: false' and 'permissions: contents: read' for least privilege. Concurrency controls implemented.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current practices. Continue SHA-pinning third-party actions and using security-conscious workflow configurations.

findings.description: GitHub Actions workflows include zizmor security scanner specifically designed to detect workflow vulnerabilities.
findings.evidence: Workflow .github/workflows/linters.yml (SHA: ccee1adde022eccc84a9b95bce82294161d4212c) includes dedicated 'zizmor' job running zizmorcore/zizmor-action@e639db99335bc9038abc0e066dfcd72e23d26fb4 with 'advanced-security: false' and 'annotations: true' configuration.
findings.recommendation: Continue using zizmor for workflow security analysis. This addresses supply chain risks specific to GitHub Actions and complements general security tooling.

findings.description: Comprehensive CI/CD pipeline with multiple quality gates including linters, tests across platforms, and code formatters.
findings.evidence: Repository contains 18 workflow files in .github/workflows/ including: linters.yml, tests.yml, coverage_tests.yml, coverage_comment.yml, check_pr_quality.yml, selenium.yml, postgis.yml. File tests.yml (SHA: b0742eba6965bfd032a7352812df463e2f4d59e3) shows Windows/SQLite tests, JavaScript tests, and script tests.
findings.recommendation: Current test coverage is strong. Consider adding security-specific test stages (SAST, dependency scanning) to complement existing quality and functional testing.

findings.description: Primary LICENSE file present with complete BSD 3-Clause license text
findings.evidence: LICENSE file found at repository root (SHA: 5f4f225dd282aa7e4361ec3c2750bbbaaed8ab1f, 1552 bytes). Content: 'Copyright (c) Django Software Foundation and individual contributors. All rights reserved. Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions are met...' - Complete BSD 3-Clause license with proper copyright attribution.
findings.recommendation: No action required. License file is properly structured and legally valid.

findings.description: License correctly declared in pyproject.toml using SPDX identifier
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml (SHA: c64cd28dab46fc6f1a24ef3e963cd6bd54186c93) contains: 'license = "BSD-3-Clause"' and 'license-files = ["LICENSE", "LICENSE.python", "AUTHORS"]'. SPDX-compliant identifier used, enabling machine-readable license detection.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Metadata follows modern Python packaging standards (PEP 639).

findings.description: Exemplary third-party license attribution for embedded Python stdlib code
findings.evidence: LICENSE.python file present (SHA: 2fc28e876fa1fc8c536f46b81789e2bb51b323c0, 14256 bytes). Contains complete Python Software Foundation license with statement: 'Django includes code from the Python standard library, which is licensed under the Python license, a permissive open source license.' Includes historical PSF, BeOpen, CNRI, and CWI license texts as required by Python license terms.
findings.recommendation: No action required. This represents best-practice compliance for third-party code attribution.

findings.description: AUTHORS file present and properly referenced in package metadata
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows AUTHORS file (45163 bytes, SHA: 07c87068a9d3b46491391818053839dcd5ffcf1e). Referenced in pyproject.toml license-files declaration alongside LICENSE and LICENSE.python.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Proper contributor recognition maintained.

findings.description: Source files do not contain per-file license headers
findings.evidence: Code search for 'repo:django/django "Copyright (c) Django Software Foundation" path:*.py' returned 0 results. Sample file django/__init__.py (SHA: 4402600c576fab7d8e88389799558aa963369b2d) examined - no copyright header present. Repository tree shows 28 top-level items including django/ source directory.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding SPDX license headers to source files (e.g., '# SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause'). While not required for BSD licenses, headers improve machine-readability for SBOM tools and clarify licensing at the file level. This is optional but recommended for enterprise compliance workflows.

findings.description: All declared dependencies use licenses compatible with BSD 3-Clause
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml dependencies section shows: 'asgiref>=3.9.1' (BSD 3-Clause), 'sqlparse>=0.5.0' (BSD 3-Clause), 'tzdata; sys_platform == "win32"' (Public Domain/Apache 2.0). Optional dependencies: 'argon2-cffi>=23.1.0' (MIT), 'bcrypt>=4.1.1' (Apache 2.0). All are permissive licenses with no copyleft restrictions.
findings.recommendation: No action required. No license compatibility conflicts detected. Continue monitoring dependencies for license changes.

findings.description: Multiple license documentation files properly organized and cross-referenced
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows LICENSE, LICENSE.python, AUTHORS, CONTRIBUTING.rst, and README.rst files. pyproject.toml declares all license files in 'license-files' field. Clear documentation structure visible from root level.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Repository demonstrates high transparency in legal documentation.

findings.description: Django uses an external issue tracker (Trac) instead of GitHub Issues. The repository has 0 open/closed GitHub issues, which is by design.
findings.evidence: list_issues returned totalCount: 0 for both open and closed states. CONTRIBUTING.rst (SHA: 4b2ab363660df5b4b43e8e263b70ddb96b1db54e) states: 'Django uses Trac to keep track of bugs, feature requests, and associated patches because GitHub doesn't provide adequate tooling for its community. Warning: non-trivial pull requests (anything more than fixing a typo) without Trac tickets will be closed!'
findings.recommendation: Current approach is working well. Consider documenting the Trac ticket requirement in a PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md for additional visibility to new contributors.

findings.description: All recent commits reference Trac ticket numbers, demonstrating tight integration between issue tracking and code contributions.
findings.evidence: From list_commits: commit 61a62be313 'Fixed #37057', commit 63c56cda13 'Fixed #35870', commit dc467fdc3b 'Fixed #36991', commit e1bdebc84e 'Fixed #35943', commit 84db026228 'Fixed #15759'. All 10 sampled commits from April 2026 include ticket references.
findings.recommendation: Continue enforcing the Trac ticket requirement. This is a best practice for traceability.

findings.description: Committer activity is concentrated on 2-3 core maintainers (jacobtylerwalls, sarahboyce) who merge contributions, though authorship is diverse.
findings.evidence: From list_commits (10 most recent): jacobtylerwalls appears as committer in 5/10 commits (IDs: 61a62be313, 63c56cda13, dc467fdc3b, e1bdebc84e, 84db026228), sarahboyce in 1 commit. However, authors are diverse: charettes (ID: 9293), annalauraw (ID: 44520920), DineshThumma9 (ID: 160909147), aadeina (ID: 100243770), cliffordgama (ID: 53076065), felixxm (ID: 2865885), jacobtylerwalls (ID: 38668450).
findings.recommendation: Continue mentoring and promoting new committers to reduce bus factor risk. Current model (trusted committers merging diverse contributions) is healthy but could be expanded.

findings.description: Django maintains an extensive AUTHORS file documenting contributor recognition over the project's lifetime.
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows AUTHORS file with size 45,163 bytes (SHA: 07c87068a9d3b46491391818053839dcd5ffcf1e), indicating comprehensive contributor acknowledgment over many years.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining the AUTHORS file. This is excellent practice for community recognition.

findings.description: Recent commits show co-authorship patterns, indicating collaborative contribution model.
findings.evidence: Commit 63c56cda13 includes 'Co-Authored-By: Marijke Luttekes <mail@marijkeluttekes.dev>' and commit e1bdebc84e includes multiple co-authors: 'Peter van der Does <peter@oneilinteractive.com>' and 'Jacob Walls <jacobtylerwalls@gmail.com>'.
findings.recommendation: Excellent practice. Continue encouraging co-authorship attribution for collaborative work.

findings.description: Django has comprehensive contribution guidelines pointing to extensive documentation.
findings.evidence: CONTRIBUTING.rst file present (1,115 bytes, SHA: 4b2ab363660df5b4b43e8e263b70ddb96b1db54e) directs contributors to 'docs/internals/contributing/' and references Code of Conduct at https://www.djangoproject.com/conduct/. File states: 'As a contributor, you can help us keep the Django community open and inclusive. Please read and follow our Code of Conduct.'
findings.recommendation: Exemplary. No changes needed.

findings.description: Extensive governance documentation exists covering organization structure, release processes, security policies, and community communication.
findings.evidence: From get_file_contents on docs/internals/: organization.txt (13,249 bytes, SHA: a213794ad36306aaf41186534b2a3b12eb6ec34e), release-process.txt (10,098 bytes, SHA: 23c1dc96222f86a896ce4dc82ea3a01b0d3489b6), security.txt (24,462 bytes, SHA: 3dd811fcd0eecd1b3a66768f5ed19be624bb5c74), mailing-lists.txt (3,299 bytes, SHA: 21a7961c41b5ddfb3ad7a913f2624e31d49df397), and contributing/ subdirectory (SHA: a4d46e490ade78c0e5d5c1dc35e42acdd84c317e).
findings.recommendation: World-class governance documentation. This is a model for other OSS projects.

findings.description: Django maintains comprehensive licensing documentation.
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows LICENSE (1,552 bytes, SHA: 5f4f225dd282aa7e4361ec3c2750bbbaaed8ab1f) and LICENSE.python (14,256 bytes, SHA: 2fc28e876fa1fc8c536f46b81789e2bb51b323c0) files at repository root.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining clear licensing. No changes needed.

findings.description: Discussion engagement cannot be directly observed from GitHub as Django uses external communication channels (Trac, mailing lists). GitHub native discussion features are not utilized.
findings.evidence: list_issues returned 0 issues (both open/closed). CONTRIBUTING.rst references external systems: Trac at https://code.djangoproject.com/newticket and mailing-lists.txt (3,299 bytes) documents community communication channels. No GitHub Discussions visible in repository.
findings.recommendation: Consider enabling GitHub Discussions as a supplementary channel for community questions and async discussion, while maintaining Trac for formal issue tracking. This could lower barriers for new contributors.

findings.description: Active development indicated by recent commits with issue resolution, suggesting healthy engagement on external platforms.
findings.evidence: 10 commits sampled from April 2026 (dates: April 22, 20, 19, 18) show continuous activity. Commits include substantive fixes ('Fixed #37057 -- Adjusted UniqueConstraint handling', 'Fixed #35870 -- Made blank choice label in forms more accessible') indicating active issue triage and resolution.
findings.recommendation: Current engagement model appears healthy. Continue monitoring response times on Trac and mailing lists.

findings.description: Django maintains extensive release process documentation and structured release notes.
findings.evidence: From docs/internals/: release-process.txt (10,098 bytes, SHA: 23c1dc96222f86a896ce4dc82ea3a01b0d3489b6), howto-release-django.txt (31,165 bytes, SHA: fabf1331cc0bea666326a374bfb4e1bd189f2390). docs/releases/ directory exists (SHA: 9274efb7e0910ce0496f325e7e8ceb20d9743fc5). deprecation.txt (55,582 bytes, SHA: c8e467a0ee2217860015da4c1a3f978817555790) documents deprecation timeline.
findings.recommendation: Excellent release management. Consider adding a top-level CHANGELOG.md that links to docs/releases/ for easier discoverability by new users.

findings.description: Continuous development activity with daily commits in recent period.
findings.evidence: list_commits shows commits from April 22, 2026 (multiple), April 20, April 19, and April 18, 2026. Most recent commit SHA: 61a62be313e395ce1265132bfc99f51476fb3c95 dated 2026-04-22T21:28:35Z. Commits show substantive work (constraint fixes, accessibility improvements, security fixes) not just maintenance.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current cadence. This indicates healthy, active project maintenance.

findings.description: Django uses modern development tooling and automation.
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows .github/ directory (SHA: 8a6f92a4463d007caa298ed4c7eb1de5f5efad61), .pre-commit-config.yaml (797 bytes), biome.json (1,699 bytes), tox.ini (2,195 bytes), .flake8 (297 bytes), and zizmor.yml (383 bytes) indicating CI/CD, code quality, and security automation.
findings.recommendation: Strong infrastructure. Continue investing in automation to support contributor productivity.

findings.description: README.rst provides comprehensive project orientation with excellent coverage of installation, usage, deployment, and contribution pathways. The file is 2,017 bytes and uses reStructuredText format with clear sections for getting started, getting help, contributing, running tests, and supporting development.
findings.evidence: File: README.rst (SHA: b7532e6b5ad352fe5d560962f8c59356d2424fae, 2017 bytes). Content includes: 'All documentation is in the "docs" directory and online at https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/stable/', points to docs/intro/install.txt for installation, docs/intro/tutorial01.txt through tutorial08.txt for tutorials, docs/howto/deployment/index.txt for deployment, and docs/internals/contributing/ for contribution guidelines. Includes links to Django Discord community, Django Forum, and fundraising information.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding status badges (CI, coverage, PyPI version) to enhance quick status visibility for new visitors.

findings.description: Exceptional documentation infrastructure with 778 files in the docs/ directory organized into a professional four-tier structure: introductory tutorials, conceptual topics, how-to guides, and complete API reference. The project uses Sphinx documentation system with ReadTheDocs integration.
findings.evidence: Repository tree scan shows 778 files in docs/ directory (recursive=true). Key directories: docs/intro/ (8 tutorial files), docs/topics/ (conceptual guides for auth, cache, db, forms, http, i18n, testing, etc.), docs/howto/ (deployment, custom commands, logging, static files, etc.), docs/ref/ (complete API reference for models, forms, templates, middleware, settings, signals, etc.). Files: .readthedocs.yml, docs/conf.py (14,810 bytes), docs/Makefile confirm Sphinx setup. Online docs at https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/stable/.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. Documentation structure is exemplary.

findings.description: Source code contains comprehensive docstrings with detailed descriptions, parameter explanations, and usage examples. Sample file django/core/management/__init__.py demonstrates consistent documentation patterns throughout the codebase.
findings.evidence: File: django/core/management/__init__.py. Functions include detailed docstrings: find_commands() - 'Given a path to a management directory, return a list of all the command names that are available.', get_commands() - 'Return a dictionary mapping command names to their callback applications...' with multi-paragraph explanation and caching details, call_command() - extensive docstring with 4 usage examples including 'call_command("migrate")', 'call_command("shell", plain=True)', etc. Class ManagementUtility includes docstring 'Encapsulate the logic of the django-admin and manage.py utilities.'
findings.recommendation: No action needed. Inline documentation quality is excellent.

findings.description: No conventional CHANGELOG.md file exists at the repository root. However, the project maintains extremely comprehensive release notes in docs/releases/ directory with 450+ individual release files covering versions from 0.95 to 6.1, plus dedicated security documentation.
findings.evidence: Search for 'filename:CHANGELOG' returned 0 results. Alternative: docs/releases/ directory contains extensive release documentation including docs/releases/index.txt (6,172 bytes), docs/releases/security.txt (70,747 bytes), major version files like docs/releases/6.0.txt (25,030 bytes), docs/releases/5.2.txt (17,839 bytes), and patch releases docs/releases/5.2.1.txt through docs/releases/5.2.13.txt. Historical coverage includes docs/releases/0.95.txt (5,716 bytes) from 2006.
findings.recommendation: Add a root-level CHANGELOG.md file that consolidates recent major releases (last 3-5 versions) in Keep a Changelog format, with links to docs/releases/ for complete history. This would improve discoverability for developers who expect changelog at repository root.

findings.description: Multi-tiered contribution documentation with root CONTRIBUTING.rst file providing quick overview and extensive guides in docs/internals/contributing/ covering workflow, coding standards, testing, documentation writing, and ticket triaging. Includes Code of Conduct reference and clear Trac ticket requirement for non-trivial contributions.
findings.evidence: File: CONTRIBUTING.rst (SHA: 4b2ab363660df5b4b43e8e263b70ddb96b1db54e, 1,115 bytes) states 'Examples of contributions include: Code patches, Documentation improvements, Bug reports and patch reviews' and warns 'non-trivial pull requests (anything more than fixing a typo) without Trac tickets will be closed!' Extended documentation found in docs/internals/contributing/ including: index.txt, new-contributors.txt (6,868 bytes), bugs-and-features.txt, writing-code/coding-style.txt (14,580 bytes), writing-code/unit-tests.txt (22,466 bytes), writing-code/submitting-patches.txt (22,412 bytes), writing-documentation.txt (23,514 bytes), triaging-tickets.txt (24,859 bytes). AUTHORS file (45,163 bytes) lists all contributors.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. Contributing documentation is comprehensive and well-organized.

findings.description: Production-quality 8-part tutorial series teaches Django through building a real polls application. Tutorial 01 demonstrates excellent pedagogical structure with step-by-step instructions, code examples with file paths, console output examples, troubleshooting admonitions, and progressive disclosure linking to next steps.
findings.evidence: File: docs/intro/tutorial01.txt (10,844 bytes) contains 'Writing your first Django app, part 1' with structured content: environment verification ('$ python -m django --version'), project creation ('$ django-admin startproject mysite djangotutorial'), directory structure explanation, development server startup, and first view creation. Includes code blocks with captions like 'polls/views.py' and 'polls/urls.py', console output examples, and admonitions ('If you get an error page here, check that you're going to http://localhost:8000/polls/'). Series continues through tutorial02.txt to tutorial08.txt. Additional examples in docs/topics/db/examples/ directory (many_to_many.txt, many_to_one.txt, one_to_one.txt). Screenshots in docs/intro/_images/ enhance visual learning.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. Tutorial quality is exceptional and serves as model for other open-source projects.

findings.description: Django demonstrates enterprise-grade documentation infrastructure with professional tooling (Sphinx, ReadTheDocs), comprehensive coverage across all user types (beginners to core developers), internationalization support, accessibility guidelines, and extensive internal documentation for release management and governance.
findings.evidence: Complete documentation ecosystem includes: .readthedocs.yml configuration, docs/_theme/ with custom styling, docs/_ext/ with custom Sphinx extensions (djangodocs.py 14,448 bytes, github_links.py 4,728 bytes), docs/man/ with man pages (django-admin.1 at 83,500 bytes), docs/internals/ with organization.txt (13,249 bytes) and release-process.txt (10,098 bytes), docs/topics/i18n/ for internationalization with translation.txt (79,961 bytes), docs/internals/contributing/accessibility.txt (6,989 bytes), and comprehensive coverage of all framework features across 778 documentation files.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. This represents best-in-class documentation for an open-source framework.
